perturbation.original_text: Women.com shall make advertising reports generated by NetGravity available to eDiets either online or in some other mutually agreed upon format. Such reports shall contain the number of impressions and other metrics attributable to each general category of advertising specified in Exhibit B hereto, as well as such other information as eDiets may reasonably request, to the extent reasonable commercial efforts and current technology permit. eDiets agrees to accept such advertising reports provided by Women.com as the official basis for measuring all services to be provided under this Agreement, provided, however, that if the traffic data contained in such reports materially differs (meaning a differential of 25% or more), from the data captured and reported by eDiets, the parties agree to either (a) identify the source of and remedy the difference or (b) equally split the difference between the traffic data reported by each party. If a technological malfunction causes the data reported by NetGravity to materially inflate the number of impressions provided eDiets by Women.com, Women.com shall deliver eDiets an amount of impressions equal to the overcount in accordance with the provisions for under-delivery set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion] hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Women.com shall provide advertising reports to eDiets. Such reports shall contain the number of impressions and other metrics attributable to each general category of advertising, as well as such other information as eDiets may reasonably request. eDiets agrees to accept such advertising reports provided by Women.com as the basis for measuring all services to be provided under this Agreement, provided, however, that if the traffic data contained in such reports materially differs from the data captured and reported by eDiets, the parties agree to identify the source of and remedy the difference. If a technological malfunction causes the data reported to materially inflate the number of impressions provided eDiets by Women.com, Women.com shall deliver eDiets an amount of impressions equal to the overcount.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific reference to 'NetGravity' and the 'mutually agreed upon format,' the agreement loses a concrete source and methodology for reporting. The removal of 'meaning a differential of 25% or more' before the dispute resolution clause significantly reduces the objectivity on deciding a dispute, and weakens the criteria for reconciliation if there's a material difference in traffic data. This creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes over the validity and accuracy of the reports, as well as when reconciliation actions are triggered, especially concerning the definition of 'materially differs'. By removing the cross-reference to Section 3.1, it creates a contradiction because the obligation to 'deliver an amount of impressions equal to the overcount' lacks a mechanism to do so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Women.com shall provide advertising reports to eDiets. Such reports shall contain the number of impressions and other metrics attributable to each general category of advertising, as well as such other information as eDiets may reasonably request. eDiets agrees to accept such advertising reports provided by Women.com as the basis for measuring all services to be provided under this Agreement, provided, however, that if the traffic data contained in such reports materially differs from the data captured and reported by eDiets, the parties agree to identify the source of and remedy the difference. If a technological malfunction causes the data reported to materially inflate the number of impressions provided eDiets by Women.com, Women.com shall deliver eDiets an amount of impressions equal to the overcount.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the objective '25% or more' threshold for defining a material difference in traffic data, as well as the specific reference to Section 3.1. Because both the changed text and the contradicted text are identical, but the context is different based on the initial, the changed text contradicts the original obligation regarding the overcount, as the removal of the reference makes the application of the clause less specific, creating an enforceability issue.
